title: senna acuruensis
intro: Senna acuruensis is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, known for its vibrant yellow flowers and distinctive, flat seed pods. The plant is a medicinal herb, traditionally used for its purgative properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of anthraquinone glycosides such as sennosides. Senna acuruensis is often used to treat constipation and other digestive issues, as its anthraquinones stimulate bowel movements and promote the elimination of waste. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or capsule, although it is also sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
